reforms.description: Austria has made a welcome step forward in tax co-operation by endorsing the OECD standard for the exchange of information in tax matters, which will enhance transparency and compliance in tax administration.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improved tax transparency can enhance the business environment, potentially attracting more investment and fostering innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: Austria is encouraged to implement this decision as rapidly as possible.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift towards greater tax transparency, which is crucial for improving the investment climate and fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Austria has made a welcome step forward in tax co-operation by endorsing the OECD standard for the exchange of information in tax matters.

reforms.description: Austria has implemented a variety of public-private cooperation schemes, direct subsidies to innovation projects, and a generous tax treatment of business R&D spending to promote technological awareness and the diffusion of innovations.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: These measures are designed to enhance innovation capacity and productivity, which can lead to higher economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The measures have been put in place and are contributing to innovation.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant shift in how Austria supports innovation, with broad implications for the economy's productivity and competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: A relatively generous tax treatment of business R&D spending, direct subsidies to innovation projects and a variety of public-private co-operation schemes have been put in place.

reforms.description: The Austrian government has extended the 'combination wage' scheme, which subsidizes part-time jobs for low-skilled workers, to include more specific groups of long-term unemployed, disabled, and older workers.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By subsidizing wages for low-skilled workers, the reform aims to increase employment rates and stimulate economic activity, thereby supporting growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is extending its reach from July 2009.
reforms.importance_rationale: The extension of the combination wage scheme represents a significant adjustment to existing employment support measures, targeting vulnerable groups to enhance their labor market participation.
reforms.source_quote: The government is extending its reach from July 2009. Eligibility will still be limited to specific groups of long-term unemployed (disabled, older workers above 50 and women entering the labour market).

reforms.description: The Austrian government has introduced a support scheme for long-term unemployed individuals, providing subsidies to enterprises that hire them, which can cover up to 100% of the gross wage in special cases.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This reform aims to reduce unemployment among vulnerable groups by incentivizing businesses to hire long-term unemployed individuals, thus fostering economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Enterprises are subsidised for hiring long-term unemployed persons.
reforms.importance_rationale: The introduction of this support scheme is a significant measure aimed at addressing long-term unemployment, which is a pressing issue in the labor market.
reforms.source_quote: Enterprises are subsidised for hiring long-term unemployed persons (defined as those who have remained unemployed for more than one year).

reforms.description: The government plans to merge the Science Council and the Council for Research and Technology to enhance cooperation and achieve more coherent policy advice, thereby increasing spending efficiency in innovation policy.
reforms.component_name: Merger of Science Council and Research Council
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving the coherence and efficiency of innovation policy is expected to enhance overall innovation outcomes and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The new government programme explicitly mentions the need to redefine the role of the Council for Research and Technology.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant structural change in the governance of innovation policy, aiming to streamline decision-making and enhance the effectiveness of public funding for research and development.
reforms.source_quote: Merge the Science Council and the Council for Research and Technology or enhance cooperation between them, to achieve more coherent policy advice, and strengthen its influence on policies in order to increase spending efficiency.

reforms.description: The government is conducting an evaluation of the existing public incentive system for research, development, and innovation to ensure the efficiency of R&D subsidies, including tax incentives.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Regular evaluations of R&D subsidies are expected to enhance their effectiveness, leading to better allocation of resources and increased innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The ongoing evaluation exercise is targeting this need.
reforms.importance_rationale: This evaluation is a significant step towards improving the effectiveness of existing innovation policies, although it does not constitute a major structural reform.
reforms.source_quote: Ensure the efficiency of R&D subsidies including tax incentives by regular independent evaluations.

reforms.description: The government aims to strengthen links between public research centres and the business sector to ensure the diffusion of innovation generated in public research.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing collaboration between public research institutions and the private sector is expected to facilitate the transfer of knowledge and technology, thereby boosting innovation and economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Improving the co-operation between public research institutions and the business sector has been at the core of innovation policies over the past decade.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant for enhancing knowledge transfer but does not represent a major overhaul of the innovation system.
reforms.source_quote: Further strengthen links between public research centres and the business sector to ensure diffusion of innovation generated in public research.

reforms.description: Austria is simplifying and reducing the cost of firm creation by authorizing electronic legal submissions and creating a 'one-stop-shop' for start-ups, alongside preparing an amendment to reduce minimum capital requirements.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By lowering barriers to entry for new firms, the reform is expected to enhance entrepreneurship and stimulate economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: Several steps were recently taken to facilitate firm creation.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant change in the regulatory environment for start-ups, which can lead to increased entrepreneurial activity.
reforms.source_quote: Several steps were recently taken to facilitate firm creation, including the authorisation of electronic legal submissions by Austrian notaries.

reforms.description: The law on small and medium-size enterprises was amended to facilitate the entry of third-party investors in their capital, aiming to create new structures for venture capital funds.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving access to venture capital, the reform is expected to enhance funding opportunities for innovative start-ups, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The law on small and medium-size enterprises was amended in 2007 to facilitate the entry of third-party investors.
reforms.importance_rationale: This amendment is a significant step towards improving the investment landscape for SMEs, which is crucial for fostering innovation.
reforms.source_quote: The law on small and medium-size enterprises was amended in 2007 in order to facilitate the entry of third-party investors in their capital.

reforms.description: Austria has initiated measures to strengthen pre-school education, including early language screening and support programmes in kindergartens, and making one year of pre-school education compulsory for all five-year-old children starting in September 2010.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improving early education is expected to enhance children's development and future educational outcomes, thereby contributing to a more skilled workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: The government considers the projects that are being launched in these areas as top priorities of its term.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant step towards improving early childhood education, which is crucial for long-term educational success and economic productivity.
reforms.source_quote: Measures have started to be taken to reinforce pre-school education, notably by introducing early language screening and support programmes in kindergarten.

reforms.description: The Austrian government has committed to overcoming premature streaming in education by creating a new comprehensive school category called the 'New Secondary School', which aims to unify academic and general education streams.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By reducing early academic streaming, the reform aims to provide equal educational opportunities, which can enhance overall educational attainment and workforce skills.
reforms.status_evidence: The government set out to unify the 'general' and 'academic' lower secondary education streams.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it addresses a critical issue in the education system, aiming to improve equity and educational outcomes for all students.
reforms.source_quote: The downsides of the early streaming of students into 'academic' and 'general' routes at the excessively young age of 10 are now recognised in Austria.

reforms.description: The Austrian government plans to strengthen Polytechnische Schulen by rearranging financial support to apprentice-taking companies, introducing new per capita subsidies based on training sophistication, and additional incentives for companies that start apprenticeships or improve training quality.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Enhancing the quality of vocational training increases the skill level of the workforce, which can lead to higher productivity and innovation in the economy.
reforms.status_evidence: The government intends to strengthen the Polytechnische Schulen.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant shift in vocational training support, aiming to improve the quality of training and increase the number of apprentices, which is crucial for addressing skill shortages.
reforms.source_quote: The government also intends to strengthen the Polytechnische Schulen.

reforms.description: A new Law on Universities was adopted to make universities autonomous institutions, providing them with stable funding sources and requiring transparency and accountability through regular reporting.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By granting autonomy and stable funding, universities can better manage resources and focus on research and innovation, ultimately contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: A new Law on Universities was adopted in 2002 and implemented from 2004.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform fundamentally changes the governance and funding structure of universities, promoting autonomy and accountability, which are essential for fostering innovation and research capacity.
reforms.source_quote: A new Law on Universities was adopted in 2002 and implemented from 2004.

reforms.description: The Austrian government committed €1 billion to support SMEs in financing investment and infrastructure projects, including €105 million from the budget and additional guarantees.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This funding is aimed at enhancing the capacity of SMEs to invest and grow, which is crucial for overall economic recovery and innovation.
reforms.status_evidence: The government committed to provide €1 billion via the SME fund.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant investment in the SME sector, which is vital for innovation and economic growth, but it is not a structural overhaul.
reforms.source_quote: The government committed to provide €1 billion (around 0.4% of GDP) via the SME fund administered by the Austrian Economic Service (AWS).

reforms.description: A second package was passed in November 2008, which included new infrastructure and R&D spending to stimulate the economy.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Investing in R&D and infrastructure is expected to enhance productivity and innovation capacity in the long run.
reforms.status_evidence: The second package was passed in November 2008, including new infrastructure and R&D spending.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform is significant as it directly allocates funds for R&D, which is crucial for innovation, but does not represent a fundamental change in policy.
reforms.source_quote: A second package was passed in November 2008. It included accelerated depreciation for equipment purchased in 2009-10, a mandatory year of kindergarten for children at age five, starting in autumn 2009 and new infrastructure and R&D spending.
